A L V Mc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/ALVM

has super-classes
Scaling c

AbsoluteFilteringc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/9VWX

This filter substitutes each original pixel value with its absolute value
has super-classes
Image Filter Space c

Acquisition Propertiesc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#Acquisition_Properties

has sub-classes
Dose Distribution Acquisition c, Imaging Acquisition Properties c, Radiomics Parameter Space c, Reconstruction Parameters c

AffineTransformationsc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/UC1G

This class includes all the filters which apply affine transformations (e.g. rotations) which can be applied to an image
has super-classes
Image Operations c
has sub-classes
6 Y I O c, K C S2 c

AggregationParametersc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/1XL1

Specifies how texture matrix are computed and aggregated
has super-classes
Feature Parameter Space c
has sub-classes
3 I D G c, 62 G R c, B T W3 c, I A Z D c, I T B B c, J J U I c, K O B O c, S U J T c, Z W7 Z c

ALK gene rearrangements [Interpretation] in Blood or Tissue by FISH Narrativec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/LNC/78210-2

has super-classes
L O I N C Tumor Marker Tests c

Anatomic Descriptorc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#Anatomic_Descriptor

has sub-classes
Laterality Qualifier c, Location Qualifier c, Radiotherapy Location Qualifier c

ApproximateVolumec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/YEKZ

In clinical practice, volumes are commonly determined by counting voxels. For volumes consisting of a large number of voxels (1000s), the differences between approximate volume and mesh-based volume are usually negligible. However for volumes with a low number of voxels (10s to 100s), approximate volume will overestimate volume compared to mesh-based volume. It is therefore only used as a reference feature, and not in the calculation of other morphological features.
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

AreaDensityAABBc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/R59B

Area Density considers the ratio of the ROI surface area and the surface area of the axis-aligned bounding box enclosing the ROI mesh vertex set. See also https://doi.org/10.1016/j.radonc.2016.07.007
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

AreaDensityAEEc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/RDD2

The area density approximate enclosing elipsoide is defined as the ratio between the area and the surface of the elipsoide having as axes the 3 eigenvectors from PCA. The surface of the elipsoide is approximated using infinite series
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

AreaDensityCHc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/7T7F

The area density convex hull is defined as the ratio between the area and the surface of the convex hull obtained by summing the sum of the area of the faces in the convex hull
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

AreaDensityMVEEc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/BRI8

The surface density minimum volume enclosing ellipsoid is defined as the ratio between the area and the surface of the eilpsoide as defined in feature VolumeDensityMVEE
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

AreaDensityOMBBc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/IQYR

The area density oriented bounding box is the ratio between the area and the surface area of the same bounding box as calculated for the VolumeDensityOMBB
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

Asphericityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/25C7

Asphericity describes how much the ROI deviates from a perfect sphere
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

B T W3c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/BTW3

has super-classes
AggregationParameters c

ButterworthFilterc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/UHZT

This filter applies a Butterworth digital algorithm to an image. It can be both a low-pass or high-pass filter. The cut-off frequency needs to be specified
has super-classes
Image Filter Space c

Cancer Patientc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#CancerPatient

A patient who has been diagnosed with or is receiving medical treatment for a malignant growth or tumor.
Current version:
STU4
Is defined by
This class denotes the Cancer Patient Profile from mCODE STU4.
has super-classes
Patient c
is in domain of
Race dp, diagnosed with op, has Death Date dp, has Patient Identifier dp, has birthsex op, has disease status op, has functional status evaluated by op, has gender op, has history of op, has measurement observation op, has recorded op, has treatment outcome op, hasBirthDate dp, hasContactInformation dp, hasName dp, hasZIPCode dp, hsa genomics report op, identified with a op, suffers from op, undergoes an op, underwent op

Cancer Risk Assessmentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#mCR_0000270

Cancer Risk Assessment acts as a standardized container for storing and exchanging estimated risk values. Because specific risk methodologies vary drastically by cancer type (e.g., leukemias versus sarcomas), this profile leaves the exact "method" open to clinical interpretation, but strictly standardizes how that data is communicated.
has super-classes
Observation c
has sub-classes
Rhabdomyosarcoma Risk Assessment c, Risk assessment for acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL). c

Cancer Risk Assessment Valuec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#Cancer_Risk_Assessment_Value

has super-classes
Observation c
has sub-classes
260360000 c, 260362008 c, 723505004 c, 723509005 c, C102402 c, C122457 c, C122458 c

asserted by op

Clinical Measurement Methodc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#Clinical_Measurement_Method

In HL7, a clinical measurement method defines the specific technique, device, or procedure used to ascertain an observation (e.g., blood pressure measured via arterial puncture vs. automatic cuff).
has super-classes
Method c
has sub-classes
410182004 c, 433465004 c, 5880005 c, Imaging Examination c
is in range of
ssessed with op

Observation c

424122007 c, ECOG Performance Status c, KPS c, Karnosfky Perfomance Status c, Lansky Play Performance Status c

has functional status evaluated by op

N category (observable entity) c

CoefficientOfVariationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/7TET

Coefficient of variation is the ratio between the standard deviation and the mean value
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Statistical c

ColliageFilterc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/F8XY

has super-classes
Image Filter Space c

Comorbiditiesc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://snomed.info/id/398192003

has super-classes
Observation c
is in range of
suffers from op

Compactness1c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/SKGS

The Compactness defines the deviation of the ROI volume from a perfect sphere. Compactness 1 is a measure of how compact, or sphere-like or sphere like the volume is
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

Compactness2c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/BQWJ

Compactness 2 is anothere measure to describe how sphere-like the volume is
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

http://www.radlex.org/RID/RID10321
is equivalent to
R I D10321 c

Imaging Modality c

CT Acquisition Propertiesc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#ComputedTomography(CT)

Tomography using x-ray transmission and a computer algorithm to reconstruct the image
has super-classes
Imaging Acquisition Properties c
is in domain of
Exposure dp, Field of View dp, Reconstruction Filter dp, Tube Current dp, Tube Voltage dp

T category (observable entity) c

has super-classes
Radiomics Report c

Acquisition Date dp, Image Processing Technique dp, Image Studied (Identifier) dp, Radiomics Software dp, Region of Interest (ROI) dp, Registration Technique dp, Segmentation Method dp, originated from op

Diagnostic Reportc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#mCR_0000298

The findings and interpretation of diagnostic tests performed on patients and/or specimens derived from these.
has super-classes
Report c
has sub-classes
Genomics Report c, Tumor Morphology c

dic(9;20)(p11-13;q11)(wcp9+,wcp20+) [Identifier] in Blood or Tissue by FISH Nominalc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/LNC/72827-9

has super-classes
L O I N C Tumor Marker Tests c

DiscretizationParametersc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/8QU8

Grey level discretisation or quantisation of the ROI is often required to make calculation of texture features tractable (Yip and Aerts, 2016)
has super-classes
Feature Parameter Space c
has sub-classes
17 X7 c, K15 C c, M V00 c, M V01 c, Q3 R U c, S S X6 c

Disease Statusc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#Disease_Status

has super-classes
Observation c
is in domain of
disease status based on op
is in range of
has disease status op

Dose Distribution Acquisitionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#DoseDistributionPlanAcquisitionProfile

has super-classes
Acquisition Properties c
is in domain of
Date of Dose Calculation dp, Dose Calculation Algorithm dp, Dose Expressed To Material dp, Dose Grid Resolution dp, Dose Units dp, Plan Sum Bias dp, Plan Sum Registration Bias dp, Treatment Planning System dp, Used Heterogeneity Correction dp
is in range of
determined by op

Dosiomics Featuresc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#DosiomicsFeatures

Advanced field of study in radiation therapy. Instead of looking at a medical image like an X-ray or MRI, dosiomics extracts high-dimensional, minable mathematical data directly from a patient's 3D radiation dose distributions.
has super-classes
Biomarkers c
has sub-classes
Statistical c, Textural c
is in domain of
Statistical Features dp, defined by op
is in range of
includes op

Dosiomics Reportc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#DosiomicsProfile

has super-classes
Report c
is in domain of
Number of CT of Interest dp, Number of Fractions Performed dp, Number of Plans Studied dp, Numer of Fractions Performed dp, Plan Name dp, Tumor Identifier dp, determined by op, includes op
is in range of
encompasses op

E Y9 Lc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/EY9L

has super-classes
InterpolationParameters c

ECOG Performance Statusc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/LNC/89262-0

The Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) Performance Status represents the patient's functional status and is used to determine their ability to tolerate therapies in serious illness, specifically for chemotherapy.
is equivalent to
424122007 c
has super-classes
Clinical Test Physical c

Elongationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/Q3CK

Elongation is the ratio between the major and minor axis lengths. Elongation is espressed as inversed ratio: 1 means completely not elongated (e.g. a sphere). Smaller values express greater elongation of the ROI volume
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

Energyc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/N8CA

Is defined by
IBSI
The energy is the square sum of all the gray levels associated to an image
has super-classes
Statistical c

Enolase.neuron specific [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasmac back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/LNC/15060-7

has super-classes
L O I N C Tumor Marker Tests c

IRI: http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/LNC/13659-8

L O I N C Tumor Marker Tests c

IRI: http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/LNC/14050-9

has super-classes
L O I N C Tumor Marker Tests c

Estrogen receptor [Moles/mass] in Tissuec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/LNC/14130-9

Evidence Typec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#Evidence_Type

has super-classes
Observation c
has sub-classes
Clinical Evidence c, Imaging Evidence c, Laboratory Evidence c, Pathologic Evidence c
is in range of
disease status based on op

F F W Dc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/FFWD

has super-classes
FeatureSpecificParameters c

Feature Parameter Spacec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/CINW

The feature parameter space is defined as the space that includes all the possible radiomics computation methods that can be applied to certain radiomics features. Subclasses are: aggregation re-segmentation, interpolation, features specific parameters, discretization
has super-classes
Radiomics Parameter Space c
has sub-classes
AggregationParameters c, DiscretizationParameters c, FeatureSpecificParameters c, InterpolationParameters c, ReSegmentationParameters c

FeatureSpecificParametersc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/OWOH

Container for parameters and computation methods used to derive features
has super-classes
Feature Parameter Space c
has sub-classes
0 O W5 c, 2 I Q V c, 3 S2 S c, 4 S5 Z c, 6 S L W c, 9 L Z9 c, C R A M c, D5 A R c, F F W D c, G5 M N c, G9 E V c, I J A Q c, K Q W7 c, L I F Z c, M V8 M c, P V M T c, V0 L6 c

Flatnessc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/N17B

The flatness is the ratio of the major and the least axis lengths. The flatness is expressed as an inverse ratio: 1 completely not flat; smaller values express objects which are increasingly flatter
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

G5 M Nc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/G5MN

has super-classes
FeatureSpecificParameters c

G6 R Oc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/G6RO

has super-classes
Image Operations c

G9 E Vc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/G9EV

has super-classes
FeatureSpecificParameters c

GaborFilterc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/OKUW

This filter applies a Gabor filter with a specified wavelength (in pixels) and orientation (in degrees)
has super-classes
Image Filter Space c

Gastrin [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasmac back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/LNC/15072-2

has super-classes
L O I N C Tumor Marker Tests c

GaussianFilterc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/CTNF

This filter applies a gaussian kernel with a pre-defined average value and variance to each pixel of an image It is used to blur image and remove details and noise
has super-classes
Image Filter Space c

GearyMeasurec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/NPT7

Geary’s C measures spatial autocorrelation. See also https://doi.org/10.2307%2F2986645
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

Genomic Region Studiedc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#mCR_0000286

has super-classes
Observation c

Genomic Variantc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#mCR_0000285

has super-classes
Observation c

Genomicsc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#Genomics

Any distinct, identifiable portion or characteristic of an organism's DNA. It can range in size from a complete chromosome down to a single base pair. In bioinformatics, features are the structural and functional components annotated on a genome's reference sequence.
has super-classes
Biomarkers c

Genomics Reportc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#mCR_0000249

Structured summary of genomic testing results generated from a molecular diagnostic assay (e.g., NGS panel, whole-exome sequencing)..A genomics report details the analysis of an individual's DNA or RNA, identifying genetic variations and their clinical implications. It typically includes variant classifications, associated gene-disease risks, and personalized treatment or preventative recommendations.
has super-classes
Diagnostic Report c
is in range of
hsa genomics report op

GLCM_AngularSecondMomentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/8ZQL

It represents the energy of the probability matrix. Please note that this feature is also called Energy or Uniformity.
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_Autocorrelationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/QWB0

The autocorrelation compares all possible pixel pairs and reporting the likelihood that both will be bright as a function of the distance and direction of separation
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_ClusterProminencec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/AE86

The cluster prominence is a measure of asymmetry. When the cluster prominence value is high, the image is less symmetric
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_ClusterShadec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/7NFM

The cluster shade is a measure of the skewness of the matrix and it is believed to gauge the perceptual concepts of uniformity. When the cluster age is high the image is asymmetric
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_ClusterTendencyc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/DG8W

The cluster tendency indicates into how many clusters the gray levels present in the image can be classified
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_Contrastc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/ACUI

Contrast assesses grey level variations. It is defines as in https://doi.org/10.5589/m02-004
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_Correlationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/NI2N

The correlation feature shows the linear dependence of gray level values in the cooccurence matrix
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_DifferenceEntropyc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/NTRS

The entropy for the diagonal probabilities
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_DifferenceVariancec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/D3YU

The variance for the diagonal probabilities
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_DifferentAveragec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/TF7R

Is defined by
DifferenceAverage
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_Dissimilarityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/8S9J

The dissimilarity feature describes the variation of grey levels pair in an image. Dissimilarity always ranges from 0 and 1. It has maximum values when the grey level of the reference and the neighbor pixel is at the extremes of the possibile grey levels in the the texture sample
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_FirstMeasureOfInformationCorrelationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/R8DG

The IMC1 is related to the entropy of the images and gives information on how a pixel value is correlated to its neighbourod
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_InverseDifferencec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/IB1Z

Inverse difference is a measure of homogeneity. Grey level co-occurrences with a large difference in levels are weighed less, thus lowering the total feature score. The feature score is maximal if all grey levels are the same
Is defined by
IBSI
is equivalent to
26200 c
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_InverseDifferenceMomentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/WF0Z

Same as the inverse difference feature, but with lower weigths for elements that are further from the diagonal
Is defined by
IBSI
is equivalent to
26201 c
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_InverseDifferenceMomentNormalisedc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/1QCO

Normalized version of the inverse difference moment, as suggested by https://doi.org/10.5589/m02-004
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_InverseDifferenceNormalisedc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/NDRX

Normalized inverse difference as suggested in https://doi.org/10.5589/m02-004
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_InverseVariancec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/E8JP

The inverse variance feature measures how the gray tone differences are distributed in pair elements
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_JointAveragec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/60VM

The grey level weigthed sum of joint probabilities
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_JointEntropyc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/TU9B

As defined in http://dx.doi.org/10.1109/TSMC.1973.4309314
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_JointMaximumc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/GYBY

Probability corresponding to the most common grey level co-occurence in the GCLM
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_JointVariancec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/UR99

Also called sum of squares as in http://dx.doi.org/10.1109/TSMC.1973.4309314
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_SecondMeasureOfInformationCorrelationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/JN9H

Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_SumAveragec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/ZGXS

The average for the cross-diagonal probabilities
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_SumEntropyc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/P6QZ

The entropy for the cross-diagonal probabilities
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLCM_SumVariancec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/OEEB

The variance for the cross-diagonal probabilities
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM) c

GLDM_DependenceCountEnergyc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/CAS9

Defined as second moment in Sun and Wee (1983)
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Dependence Matrix (GLDM) c

GLDM_DependenceCountEntropyc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/FCBV

The entropy for the dependence counts
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Dependence Matrix (GLDM) c

GLDM_DependenceCountNonUniformityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/Z87G

This features assesses the distribution of neighbouring grey level dependence counts over the different dependence counts. The feature value is low when dependence counts are equally distributed
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Dependence Matrix (GLDM) c

GLDM_DependenceCountNonUniformityNormalisedc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/OKJI

This is a normalised version of the dependence count non-uniformity feature
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Dependence Matrix (GLDM) c

GLDM_DependenceCountPercentagec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/6XV8

This feature assesses the fraction of the number of realised neighbourhoods and the max- imum number of potential neighbourhoods. The feature may be omitted as it evaluates to 1 when complete neighbourhoods are not required, which is the case under our definition
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Dependence Matrix (GLDM) c

GLDM_DependenceCountVariancec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/DNX2

This feature estimates the variance in dependence counts for the different dependence counts possible
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Dependence Matrix (GLDM) c

GLDM_GreyLevelNonUniformityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/FP8K

This feature assesses the distribution of neighbouring grey level dependence counts over the grey values. The feature value is low when dependence counts are equally distributed along grey levels
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes

Grey Level Run Length Matrix (GLRLM) c

GLRLM_RunPercentagec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/9ZK5

This feature assesses the fraction of the number of realised runs and the maximum number of potential runs (Galloway, 1975). Strongly linear or highly uniform ROI volumes produce a low run percentage
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Run Length Matrix (GLRLM) c

GLRLM_ShortRunEmphasisc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/22OV

The SRE measures the distribution of short runs. The SRE is highly dependent on the occurrence of short runs and it gives high value for fine texture the value of SRE is high

IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Run Length Matrix (GLRLM) c

GLRLM_ShortRunHighGreyLevelEmphasisc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/GD3A

The SRHGLE measures the joint distribution of short runs and high gray level values. The SRHGE is high for the image with many short runs and high gray level values

IBSI
has super-classes
Grey Level Run Length Matrix (GLRLM) c

Grey Level Co Occurence Matrix (GLCM)c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/LFYI

The grey level co-occurrence matrix (GLCM) is a matrix that expresses how combinations of discretised grey levels of neighbouring pixels, or voxels in a 3D volume, are distributed along one of the image directions. In a 3 dimensional approach to texture analysis, the direct neighbourhood of a voxel consists of the 26 directly neighbouring voxels. Thus, there are 13 unique direction vectors within a neighbourhood volume for distance
has super-classes
Textural c
has sub-classes
26200 c, 26201 c, GLCM_AngularSecondMoment c, GLCM_Autocorrelation c, GLCM_ClusterProminence c, GLCM_ClusterShade c, GLCM_ClusterTendency c, GLCM_Contrast c, GLCM_Correlation c, GLCM_DifferenceEntropy c, GLCM_DifferenceVariance c, GLCM_DifferentAverage c, GLCM_Dissimilarity c, GLCM_FirstMeasureOfInformationCorrelation c, GLCM_InverseDifference c, GLCM_InverseDifferenceMoment c, GLCM_InverseDifferenceMomentNormalised c, GLCM_InverseDifferenceNormalised c, GLCM_InverseVariance c, GLCM_JointAverage c, GLCM_JointEntropy c, GLCM_JointMaximum c, GLCM_JointVariance c, GLCM_SecondMeasureOfInformationCorrelation c, GLCM_SumAverage c, GLCM_SumEntropy c, GLCM_SumVariance c

Grey Level Dependence Matrix (GLDM)c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/REK0

The neighbouring grey level dependence matrix (NGLDM) captures the coarseness of the overall texture and is rotationally invariant. Defined in https://doi.org/10.1016/0734-189X(83)90032-4
has super-classes
Textural c
has sub-classes
GLDM_DependenceCountEnergy c, GLDM_DependenceCountEntropy c, GLDM_DependenceCountNonUniformity c, GLDM_DependenceCountNonUniformityNormalised c, GLDM_DependenceCountPercentage c, GLDM_DependenceCountVariance c, GLDM_GreyLevelNonUniformity c, GLDM_GreyLevelNonUniformityNormalised c, GLDM_GreyLevelVariance c, GLDM_HighDependenceEmphasis c, GLDM_HighDependenceHighGreyLevelEmphasis c, GLDM_HighDependenceLowGreyLevelEmphasis c, GLDM_HighGreyLevelCountEmphasis c, GLDM_LowDependenceEmphasis c, GLDM_LowDependenceHighGreyLevelEmphasis c, GLDM_LowDependenceLowGreyLevelEmphasis c, GLDM_LowGreyLevelCountEmphasis c

Grey Level Run Length Matrix (GLRLM)c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/TP0I

The Grey level run length matrix (GLRLM) like the grey level co-occurrence matrix, GLRLM also assesses the distribution of discretised grey levels in an image or in a stack of images. However, instead of assessing the combination of levels between neighbouring pixels or voxels, GLRLM assesses grey level run lengths. Run length counts the frequency of consecutive voxels with discretised grey level i along direction delta
has super-classes
Textural c
has sub-classes
GLRLM_GreyLevelNonUniformity c, GLRLM_GreyLevelNonUniformityNormalised c, GLRLM_GreyLevelVariance c, GLRLM_HighGreyLevelRunEmphasis c, GLRLM_LongRunEmphasis c, GLRLM_LongRunHighGreyLevelEmphasis c, GLRLM_LongRunLowGreyLevelEmphasis c, GLRLM_LowGreyLevelRunEmphasis c, GLRLM_RunEntropy c, GLRLM_RunLengthNonUniformity c, GLRLM_RunLengthNonUniformityNormalised c, GLRLM_RunLengthVariance c, GLRLM_RunPercentage c, GLRLM_ShortRunEmphasis c, GLRLM_ShortRunHighGreyLevelEmphasis c, GLRLM_ShortRunLowGreyLevelEmphasis c

Grey Level Size Zone Matrix (GLSZM)c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/9SAK

The grey level size zone matrix (GLSZM) counts the number of groups of connected voxels with a specific discretised grey level value and size as in https://doi.org/10.1109/TBME.2013.2284600. Voxels are con- nected if the neighbouring voxel has the same discretised grey level value
has super-classes
Textural c
has sub-classes
GLSZM_GreyLevelNonUniformity c, GLSZM_GreyLevelNonUniformityNormalised c, GLSZM_GreyLevelVariance c, GLSZM_HighGreyLevelZoneEmphasis c, GLSZM_LargeZoneEmphasis c, GLSZM_LargeZoneHighGreyLevelEmphasis c, GLSZM_LargeZoneLowGreyLevelEmphasis c, GLSZM_LowGreyLevelZoneEmphasis c, GLSZM_SmallZoneEmphasis c, GLSZM_SmallZoneHighGreyLevelEmphasis c, GLSZM_SmallZoneLowGreyLevelEmphasis c, GLSZM_ZonePercentage c, GLSZM_ZoneSizeEntropy c, GLSZM_ZoneSizeNonUniformity c, GLSZM_ZoneSizeNonUniformityNormalised c, GLSZM_ZoneSizeVariance c

IntegratedIntensityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/99N0

Integrated intensity is the average grey level multiplied by the volume. In the context of 18F-FDG-PET, this feature is called total legion glycolysis. See also https://doi.org/10.1016/j.radonc.2011.10.014
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

IntensityRangec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/2OJQ

The range of grey levels is the difference between the maximum and the minimum
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Statistical c

has super-classes
Histologic Grade c

InterpolationParametersc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/VTM2

Interpolation algoriths determine the grey level values in the interpolation grid after interpolation of the original grid. Interpolation is commonly used for texture features, to isotropic voxel sizes to be rotationally invariant
has super-classes
Feature Parameter Space c
has sub-classes
2 I2 N c, E Y9 L c, N V3 D c, X13 G c

InterquartileRangec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/SALO

The interquartile range (IQR) is defined as P75 - P25, where P75 is the 75th percentile of the image matrix, and P25 is the 25th
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Statistical c

Karnosfky Perfomance Statusc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/LNC/89247-1

The Karnofsky Performance Status (KPS) is a tool used to measure a patient's functional status. It can be used to compare the effectiveness of different therapies and to help assess the prognosis of certain patients, such as those with certain cancers. The KPS score ranges from 0 to 100 in intervals of 10. Higher scores are associated with better functional status, with 100 representing no symptoms or evidence of disease, and 0 representing death.
is equivalent to
KPS c
has super-classes
Clinical Test Physical c

KPSc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://snomed.info/id/273546003

has super-classes
Clinical Test Physical c

Kurtosisc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/IPH6

Kurtosis is a measure of how outlier-prone a distribution is. The kurtosis of the normal distribution is 3. Distributions that are more outlier-prone than the normal distribution have kurtosis greater than 3; distributions that are less outlier-prone have kurtosis less than 3
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes

activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Pyruvate to lactate reaction c, MAML2 11q21 gene rearrangements in Tissue by FISH c, MYC gene rearrangements [Presence] in Blood or Tissue by FISH c, dic(9;20)(p11-13;q11)(wcp9+,wcp20+) [Identifier] in Blood or Tissue by FISH Nominal c, t(4;14)(p16;q32)(FGFR3,IGH) fusion transcript [Identifier] in Blood or Tissue by FISH Nominal c

Laboratory Evidencec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#Laboratory_Evidence

has super-classes
Evidence Type c

Laboratory Testc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#mCR_0000332

Standardized, machine-readable specification for a specific clinical laboratory procedure or panel
has super-classes
Observation c
has sub-classes
122869004 c, Histologic Behavior c, Histologic Grade c, Histologic Type c, Tumor Marker Test c
is in range of
underwent op

Lactate dehydrogenase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Pyruvate to lactate reactionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/LNC/14805-6

has super-classes
L O I N C Tumor Marker Tests c

Lansky Play Performance Statusc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#mCR_0000265

The Lansky Play-Performance Status for children is a parent-rated instrument which records usual play activity as the index of performance. It is similar to the Karnofsky Performance Scale for adults
has super-classes
Clinical Test Physical c

LaplacianFilterc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/CMWE

Convolutes with a laplacian kernel the original image
has super-classes
Image Filter Space c

Laterality Qualifierc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#Laterality_Qualifier

has super-classes
Anatomic Descriptor c
has sub-classes
24028007 c, 7771000 c

LeastAxisLengthc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/7J51

The least axis is the the axis along which the object is least extended. The least axis is twice the semi-axis length, determined using the smallest eigenvalue obtained by PCA on the point set of voxel centers
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

Location Qualifierc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#Location_Qualifier

has super-classes
Anatomic Descriptor c

Low histologic grade (qualifier value)c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://snomed.info/id/1155708003

has super-classes
Histologic Grade c

Lymphoma stage (observable entity)c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://snomed.info/id/385388004

has super-classes
Cancer Stage c, Tumor stage (observable entity) c
has sub-classes
254371009 c, 254372002 c, 254375000 c, C141158 c, C141178 c, C141189 c

M category (observable entity)c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://snomed.info/id/277208005

has super-classes
Tumor-node-metastasis (TNM) category (observable entity) c
has sub-classes
cM category (observable entity) c, pM category (observable entity) c

M V00c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/MV00

has super-classes
DiscretizationParameters c

M V01c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/MV01

has super-classes
DiscretizationParameters c

M V8 Mc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/MV8M

has super-classes
FeatureSpecificParameters c

Magnetic Resonance Image (MRI)c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://ncicb.nci.nih.gov/xml/owl/EVS/Thesaurus.owl#C16809

Non-invasive method of demonstrating internal anatomy based on the principle that atomic nuclei in a strong magnetic field absorb pulses of radiofrequency energy and emit them as radiowaves which can be reconstructed into computerized images. The concept includes proton spin tomographic techniques.
Is defined by
http://www.radlex.org/RID/RID10312
http://www.radlex.org/RID/RID10312
is equivalent to
R I D10312 c
has super-classes
Imaging Modality c

MajorAxisLengthc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/TDIC

The major axis length is defined as twice the semi-axis length, dtermined using the largest eigenvalue obtained by principal component analysis (PCA) on the point set of voxel centers
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

MAML2 11q21 gene rearrangements in Tissue by FISHc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/LNC/74034-0

has super-classes
L O I N C Tumor Marker Tests c

MathOperationsc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/Z7IV

This class includes all the filters which apply math operations between two / more different images
has super-classes
Image Operations c
has sub-classes
6 H I B c, J Q Q R c, P I P E c

Maximumc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/84IY

The maximum intensity value
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Statistical c

Maximum2DDiameterColumnc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/5ABU

Maximum 2D diameter (Column) is defined as the largest pairwise Euclidean distance between tumor surface voxels in the row-slice (usually the coronal) plane.
Is defined by
Pyradiomics
has super-classes
Morphological c

Maximum2DDiameterRowc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/9SCJ

Maximum 2D diameter (Row) is defined as the largest pairwise Euclidean distance between tumor surface voxels in the column-slice (usually the sagittal) plane.
Is defined by
Pyradiomics
has super-classes
Morphological c

Maximum2DDiameterSlicec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/4S59

Maximum 2D diameter (Slice) is defined as the largest pairwise Euclidean distance between tumor surface voxels in the row-column (generally the axial) plane.
Is defined by
Pyradiomics
has super-classes
Morphological c

Maximum3DDiameterc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/L0JK

The maximum 3D diameter is the distance between the two most distant vertices in the ROI mesh vertices sets
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

Meanc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/Q4LE

The mean grey level from grey level distribution
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Statistical c

MeanAbsoluteDeviationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/4FUA

The mean of the absolute deviations of all voxel intensities around the mean intensitity value
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Statistical c

MeanFilterc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/IUBX

The mean filter works on a n x n subregion of the image. At each position the center pixel is replaced by the mean value
has super-classes
Image Filter Space c

Measurementc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#mCR_0000333

has super-classes
Observation c
has sub-classes
Blood Pressure c, Body Surface Area c, Height c, Tumor Size c, Weight c
is in range of
has measurement observation op

Medianc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/Y12H

The median intensity value
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Statistical c

MedianAbsoluteDeviationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/N72L

Median absolute deviation is similar in concept to mean absolute deviation, but measures dispersion from the median instead of mean
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Statistical c

MedianFilterc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/MBXH

The median filter works on a n x n subregion of the image. At each position the center voxel is replaced by the median value
has super-classes
Image Filter Space c

Methodc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#mCR_0000316

Specific procedure, technique, or device used to obtain a clinical observation or collect a specimen.
has sub-classes
Clinical Measurement Method c, Staging Method c, Tumor Size Method c

Minimumc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/1GSF

The minimum intensity value
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Statistical c

MinorAxisLengthc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/P9VJ

The minor axis length of the ROI provides a measure of how fare the volume extends along the second largest axis. The minor axis length is twice the semi-axis length, determined using the second largest eigenvalue obtained by PCA on the point of the voxel centers
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

MoranIndexc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/N365

Moran’s I index is an indicator of spatial autocorrelation. See also https://doi.org/10.1093/biomet/37.1-2.17
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

Morphologicalc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/HCUG

Morphological features describe geometric aspects of a region of interest (ROI), such as area and volume. Morphological features are based on ROI voxel representations of the volume.
has super-classes
Radiomics Features c
has sub-classes
ApproximateVolume c, AreaDensityAABB c, AreaDensityAEE c, AreaDensityCH c, AreaDensityMVEE c, AreaDensityOMBB c, Asphericity c, CentreOfMassShift c, Compactness1 c, Compactness2 c, Elongation c, Flatness c, GearyMeasure c, IntegratedIntensity c, LeastAxisLength c, MajorAxisLength c, Maximum2DDiameterColumn c, Maximum2DDiameterRow c, Maximum2DDiameterSlice c, Maximum3DDiameter c, MinorAxisLength c, MoranIndex c, SphericalDisproportion c, Sphericity c, SurfaceArea c, SurfaceToVolumeRatio c, Volume c, VolumeDensityAABB c, VolumeDensityAEE c, VolumeDensityCH c, VolumeDensityMVEE c, VolumeDensityOMBB c

MRI Acquisition Propertiesc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#MagneticResonanceImage(MRI)

Non-invasive method of demonstrating internal anatomy based on the principle that atomic nuclei in a strong magnetic field absorb pulses of radiofrequency energy and emit them as radiowaves which can be reconstructed into computerized images. The concept includes proton spin tomographic techniques.
has super-classes
Imaging Acquisition Properties c
is in domain of
Echo Time dp, Echo Train Length dp, Flip Angle dp, Inversion Time dp, Magnetic Strength Field dp, Number of Excitations dp, RF Coil dp, Repetition Time dp, Scanning Sequences Acquired dp, k-Space Transversal dp

MYC gene rearrangements [Presence] in Blood or Tissue by FISHc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/LNC/78215-1

has super-classes
L O I N C Tumor Marker Tests c

N category (observable entity)c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://snomed.info/id/277206009

has super-classes
Tumor-node-metastasis (TNM) category (observable entity) c
has sub-classes
cN category (observable entity) c, pN category (observable entity) c

N V3 Dc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/NV3D

has super-classes
InterpolationParameters c

Neighbourhood Grey Tone Difference Matrix (NGTDM)c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/IPET

The neighbourhood grey tone difference matrix (NGTDM) contains the sum of grey level differences of pixels/voxels with discretised grey level i and the average discretised grey level of neigh- bouring pixels/voxels within a distance d. See also https://doi.org/10.1109/21.44046
has super-classes
Textural c
has sub-classes
NGTDM_Busyness c, NGTDM_Coarseness c, NGTDM_Complexity c, NGTDM_Contrast c, NGTDM_Strength c

Neoplasm and/or hamartomac back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://snomed.info/id/399981008

has super-classes
Health Condition c
has sub-classes
Neoplastic disease c

Neoplastic diseasec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://snomed.info/id/55342001

has super-classes
Neoplasm and/or hamartoma c
has sub-classes
Primary malignant neoplasm (Primary Cancer Condition) c, Secondary malignant neoplastic disease (Secondary Cancer Condition) c

NGTDM_Busynessc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/NQ30

Textures with large changes in grey levels between neighbouring voxels are called busy
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Neighbourhood Grey Tone Difference Matrix (NGTDM) c

NGTDM_Coarsenessc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/QCDE

Grey level differences in coarse textures are generally small due to large-scale patterns. Summing differences gives an indication of the level of the spatial rate of change in intensity
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Neighbourhood Grey Tone Difference Matrix (NGTDM) c

NGTDM_Complexityc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/HDEZ

Complex textures are non-uniform and rapid changes in grey levels are common
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Neighbourhood Grey Tone Difference Matrix (NGTDM) c

NGTDM_Contrastc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/65HE

Contrast depends on the dynamic range of the grey levels as well as the spatial frequency of intensity changes
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Neighbourhood Grey Tone Difference Matrix (NGTDM) c

NGTDM_Strengthc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/1X9X

Feature defined in http://dx.doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0093600
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Neighbourhood Grey Tone Difference Matrix (NGTDM) c

O-RAWc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#Radiomics

has sub-classes
T051 c, T071 c

Observationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#mCR_0000261

Data structure used to transmit measurements, test results, and clinical assessments made about a patient
has sub-classes
Cancer Risk Assessment c, Cancer Risk Assessment Value c, Cancer Stage c, Clinical Test Physical c, Comorbidities c, Disease Status c, Evidence Type c, Genomic Region Studied c, Genomic Variant c, Imaging Result c, Laboratory Test c, Measurement c

P I P Ec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/PIPE

has super-classes
MathOperations c

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/PVMT

has super-classes
FeatureSpecificParameters c

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#Pathologic_Evidence

has super-classes

IRI: http://snomed.info/id/399588009

has super-classes

The statistical 10th percentile of the grey level distribution

IBSI
has super-classes
Statistical c

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/8DWT

The statistical 90th percentile of the grey level distribution
Is defined by
IBSI

Statistical c

I Z B Z c, Q2 Z B c, Z S1 T c

M category (observable entity) c

IRI: http://snomed.info/id/371494008

has super-classes
N category (observable entity) c

Positron Emission Tomography (PET)c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://ncicb.nci.nih.gov/xml/owl/EVS/Thesaurus.owl#C17007

An imaging technique using compounds labelled with short-lived positron-emitting radionuclides (such as carbon-11, nitrogen-13, oxygen-15 and fluorine-18) to measure cell metabolism. It has been useful in the study of soft tissues such as cancer; cardiovascular system; and the brain. SPECT is closely related to PET, but uses isotopes with longer half-lives and resolution is lower.
Is defined by
http://www.radlex.org/RID/RID10337
http://www.radlex.org/RID/RID10337
is equivalent to
R I D10337 c
has super-classes
Imaging Modality c

Primary malignant neoplasm (Primary Cancer Condition)c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://snomed.info/id/372087000

Records the primary cancer condition, the original or first neoplasm in the body (Definition from: NCI Dictionary of Cancer Terms). Cancers that are not clearly secondary (i.e., of uncertain origin or behavior) should be documented as primary.
has super-classes
Neoplastic disease c
is in range of
diagnosed with op

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#mCR_0000268

has sub-classes

QuartileCoefficientOfDispersionc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/9S40

The quartile coefficient of dispersion is defined as the ratio between the difference of the 75 and 25 percentile divided by the sume of 75 and 25 percentile
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Statistical c

R I D10311c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radlex.org/RID/RID10311

has super-classes
Rad Lex c

Racec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://snomed.info/id/415229000

is in range of
has race op

Radiomics Featuresc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/72T0

Radiomics features are particular features which can be extracted from objects within an image (e.g. from a region of interest) and that can potentially present a prognostic value (e.g related to the survival probability of a patient).
has super-classes
Biomarkers c
has sub-classes
Morphological c, Statistical c, Textural c
is in domain of
defined by op
is in range of
contains op

Radiomics Parameter Spacec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#ImageFeatureSpace

has super-classes
Acquisition Properties c
has sub-classes
Feature Parameter Space c, Image Filter Space c, Image Operations c
is in domain of
Discretization Parameters dp, Image Filter Type dp, Normalization Parameters dp, ReSegmentation Parameters dp, Resampling Parameters dp
is in range of
defined by op

Radiomics Reportc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#RadiomicsProfile

This class denotes the Radiomcis Profile for the Cancer Patient, which describes the features of the detected tumor using medical images.
has super-classes
Report c
has sub-classes
Diagnostic Radiomics Report c, Radiotherapy Radiomics Report c
is in domain of
Number of Diagnostic Images dp, Number of Images of Interest dp, Number of Therapeutic Images dp, Tumor Identifier dp, contains op

Radiotherapy Coursec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://snomed.info/id/1287742003

A course of radiotherapy delivered to a patient. It records the treatment intent, termination reason, modalities, techniques, number of sessions, and doses delivered to one or more body volumes. Whether the course has been fully delivered or stopped is indicated in the status element.
is equivalent to
Radiotherapy Course c
has super-classes
S N O M E D C T entities c
is in domain of
Body Volume dp, Number of Fractions dp, Number of Sessions dp, Radiotherapy Modality dp, Radiotherapy Technque dp, Status dp, Total Dose dp, encompasses op

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#Radiotherapy_Course

Reportc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#mCR_0000256

An evidence-based document that presents, analyzes, and summarizes medical or scientific data
has sub-classes
Diagnostic Report c, Dosiomics Report c, Radiomics Report c

Request Procedure Intentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#Request_Procedure_Intent

has super-classes
Clinical Intent c

ReSegmentationParametersc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/QP79

Re-segmentation entails updating the ROI mask R based on certain corresponding voxel intensities. Re-segmentation may be performed to exclude voxels from a previously segmented ROI, and is performed after interpolation.
has super-classes
Feature Parameter Space c
has sub-classes
7 A C A c, U S B3 c

Rhabdomyosarcoma Risk Assessmentc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#mCR_0000278

Rhabdomyosarcoma (RMS) is defined by SNOMED CT under the hierarchy of malignant neoplasms and disorders. While there is no single SNOMED code for a "risk assessment," the concept is represented through standardized clinical criteria that measure tumor severity and prognosis.
has super-classes
Cancer Risk Assessment c
has sub-classes
723505004 c, 723509005 c, C102402 c

RietsFilterc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/3YWD

has super-classes
Image Filter Space c

Risk assessment for acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL).c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.semanticweb.org/ontologies/mCODERadiomics#mCR_0000279

Standardized metrics for Risk assessment for Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (ALL)
has super-classes
Cancer Risk Assessment c
has sub-classes
C122457 c, C122458 c

RobustMeanAbsoluteDeviationc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/CAC8

The mean absolute deviation feature may be influenced by outliers. To increase robustness, the set of grey levels can be restricted to those which lie closer to the center of the distribution.
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Statistical c

RootMeanSquarec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/5ZWQ

The square root of the arithmetic mean of the squares of the values
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Statistical c

S S X6c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/SSX6

has super-classes
DiscretizationParameters c

S U J Tc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/SUJT

has super-classes
AggregationParameters c

S5 Z Ec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/S5ZE

has super-classes
Scaling c

SavitzkyGolayFilterc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/NZ9F

This filter is also called also called digital smoothing polynomial filters or least-squares smoothing filter. It is used to "smooth out" a noisy signal whose frequency span (without noise) is large. This filter minimizes the least-squares error in fitting a polynomial to frames of noisy data
has super-classes
Image Filter Space c

Scalingc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/BCE0

This class includes all the filters which are used to resize an image. They can be used both to increase or reduce the dimensions
has super-classes
Image Operations c
has sub-classes
A L V M c, C H45 c, L M S W c, S5 Z E c, W T M V c, X S B8 c, Y P6 L c, Z09 F c

Secondary malignant neoplastic disease (Secondary Cancer Condition)c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://snomed.indo/id/28462008

A malignant tumor that has spread from a primary (original) site to another location in the body, or a new malignant lesion that arises as a complication of cancer treatment
has super-classes
Neoplastic disease c
is in range of
diagnosed with op

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/KE2A

The skewness measures the degree of histogram of gray levels asymmetry around the central value

IBSI
has super-classes
Statistical c

Sphericity is a further measure to describe how sphere-like the volume is

IBSI

Variancec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/ECT3

The variance from grey level distribution
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Statistical c

Volumec back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/RNU0

The volume V is calculated from the ROI mesh as indicated in https://doi.org/10.1109/ICIP.2001.958278
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

VolumeDensityAABBc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/PBX1

Volume density is the fraction of the ROI volume and a comparison volume. This feature is also called extent See https://doi.org/10.1016/j.patcog.2008.08.011
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

VolumeDensityAEEc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/6BDE

The volume density approximate enclosing elipsoide is defined as the ratio between the volume and the volume of the elipsoide having as principal axes the eigenvenctors from the principal component analysis of the ROI
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

VolumeDensityCHc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/R3ER

The volume density convex hull is defined as the ration between the volume and the volume of the ROI mesh convex hull set. This feature is also called solidity. See also https://dx.doi.org/10.1016%2Fj.patcog.2008.08.011
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

VolumeDensityMVEEc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/SWZ1

The volume density minimum volume enclosing ellipsoid is the ratio between the volume and the minimum volume enclosing ellipsoid, calculated as in https://doi.org/10.1016/j.dam.2007.02.013
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

VolumeDensityOMBBc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/ZH1A

The volume density oriented mimum bounding box is the ratio between the volume and the volume of the oriented mimum bounding box. . The oriented minimum bounding box of the ROI mesh vertex set encloses the vertex set and has the smallest possible volume
Is defined by
IBSI
has super-classes
Morphological c

W E59c back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/WE59

has super-classes
TopologicalOperations c

W J T Jc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/WJTJ

has super-classes
TopologicalOperations c

W T M Vc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/WTMV

has super-classes
Scaling c

WaveletFilterc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/4BEG

This filter decomposes an image in wavelets. A wavelet series is a representation of a square-integrable (real- or complex-valued) function by a certain orthonormal series generated by a wavelet
has super-classes
Image Filter Space c

Weightc back to ToC or Class ToC

IRI: http://snomed.info/id/27113001

has super-classes
Measurement c

IRI: http://www.radiomics.org/RO/XSB8
